---
title: "OKRs → KPIs Mapping"
---

## Executive Tracking Framework (Strategy & Governance) {.unnumbered}

## Purpose of This Document

This document maps **[OKR (Automated Screening of AUV Images Using AI/CV)
at section @sec-okr]** to a concise set of **executive-level KPIs**, enabling:

- Strategic monitoring of value delivery
- Clear visibility of ecological and business outcomes
- Fact-based decision-making at governance and sponsor level

This artifact complements the **Strategy & Governance – Value Stream & OKRs**
document and is used for **executive reporting and steering**, not for
operational control.

---

## Guiding Principle for KPI Design

> **KPIs must reflect ecological value first, with efficiency and cost benefits
> as secondary, enabling outcomes.**

All KPIs are:

- Outcome-oriented (not task-based)
- Measurable with available data
- Interpretable by non-technical stakeholders

---

## OKR 2.1 — Automated Screening of AUV Images Using AI/CV

### Strategic Objective (Reminder)

Enable automated screening of AUV imagery through Computer Vision to reduce
human effort, analysis time, and operational cost, while **protecting ecological
relevance and environmental confidence**, with validation by NOAA and reuse
potential across SUB.

---

## KPI Mapping Overview

| OKR Component | Executive KPI | Description | Ecological / Business Focus |
|--------------|---------------|-------------|-----------------------------|
| KR1 | KPI-1: Ecologically Relevant Focus Ratio | Measures how much expert effort is concentrated on ecologically relevant data | **Ecological (Primary)** |
| KR2 | KPI-2: Environmental Screening Lead Time | Measures speed of access to ecological insights | **Ecological (Primary)** |
| KR3 | KPI-3: Ecological Data Protection Index | Measures protection of benthic-relevant data | **Ecological (Primary)** |
| KR4 | KPI-4: Cross-Field Ecological Consistency | Measures consistency across fields | **Ecological (Primary)** |
| KR5 | KPI-5: Reusable Environmental Asset Coverage | Measures long-term asset creation | **Strategic / Long-Term** |
| — | KPI-6: Expert Effort Reduction | Measures efficiency gain | Secondary (Enabler) |
| — | KPI-7: Analysis Cost per Campaign | Measures economic impact | Secondary (Enabler) |

---

## Detailed KPI Definitions

---

### KPI-1 — Ecologically Relevant Focus Ratio  

*(Primary ecological KPI)*

**Definition**  
Percentage of expert-reviewed images that are classified as benthic-relevant
after AI screening.

**Formula**  
```
(Number of benthic-relevant images reviewed by experts)
-------------------------------------------------------
(Total number of images reviewed by experts)
```

**Target (MVP)**  
≥ 70%

**Why Executives Care**

- Indicates whether expert time is being spent where ecological value exists
- Direct proxy for KR1 ecological intent

---

### KPI-2 — Environmental Screening Lead Time  

*(Primary ecological KPI)*

**Definition**  
Elapsed time between AUV image acquisition and availability of ecologically
relevant screening results to NOAA.

**Target (MVP)**  
≥ 25% reduction compared to manual baseline

**Why Executives Care**

- Faster environmental insight
- Improved responsiveness for monitoring, licensing, and assessments

---

### KPI-3 — Ecological Data Protection Index  

*(Primary ecological risk-control KPI)*

**Definition**  
Retention rate of true benthic-relevant images after automated screening.

**Formula**  
```
(True benthic-relevant images retained)
--------------------------------------
(Total true benthic-relevant images)
```

**Target (MVP)**  
≥ 85–90%

**Why Executives Care**

- Directly controls ecological risk
- Protects credibility of environmental studies

---

### KPI-4 — Cross-Field Ecological Consistency  

*(Primary ecological scalability KPI)*

**Definition**  
Degree to which screening performance (precision/retention) remains stable
across different fields (e.g., Viola vs Espadarte).

**Target (MVP)**  
Variation ≤ agreed tolerance threshold between fields

**Why Executives Care**

- Ensures environmental consistency
- Reduces field-specific bias and rework

---

### KPI-5 — Reusable Environmental Asset Coverage  

*(Strategic, long-term KPI)*

**Definition**  
Percentage of screened images and associated metadata consolidated into
versioned, auditable corporate repositories.

**Target (MVP)**  
≥ 90% of processed data assets versioned and reusable

**Why Executives Care**

- Confirms creation of long-term environmental digital assets
- Enables reuse beyond the initial project

---

### KPI-6 — Expert Effort Reduction  

*(Secondary, enabling KPI)*

**Definition**  
Reduction in total specialist hours required for initial screening compared to
manual baseline.

**Target (MVP)**  
≥ 30% reduction

**Why Executives Care**

- Confirms operational efficiency
- Supports sustainability of expert workforce

---

### KPI-7 — Analysis Cost per Campaign  

*(Secondary, enabling KPI)*

**Definition**  
Average cost of image screening per AUV campaign.

**Target (MVP)**  
Downward trend demonstrated (absolute value contextual)

**Why Executives Care**

- Demonstrates economic sustainability
- Supports scaling decisions

---

## Executive Dashboard Recommendation

For executive steering, KPIs should be grouped as:

### Environmental Confidence Panel (Primary)

- KPI-1: Ecologically Relevant Focus Ratio
- KPI-2: Environmental Screening Lead Time
- KPI-3: Ecological Data Protection Index
- KPI-4: Cross-Field Ecological Consistency

### Sustainability & Efficiency Panel (Secondary)

- KPI-6: Expert Effort Reduction
- KPI-7: Analysis Cost per Campaign

### Strategic Asset Panel

- KPI-5: Reusable Environmental Asset Coverage

---

## Governance Usage

- Reviewed at **executive checkpoints**
- Used for **Go / No-Go decisions**
- Forms the basis for scaling beyond MVP
- KPIs are reported without exposing low-level technical metrics

---

## Binding Rule

> **No decision to scale, reuse, or operationalize the solution may be taken
> without KPI-3 (Ecological Data Protection Index) meeting its minimum
> threshold.**

---

## Summary for Executives

This KPI framework ensures that:

- Ecological value is always visible and prioritized
- Automation is measured by outcomes, not activity
- Scaling decisions are evidence-based and risk-aware

This document is the authoritative reference for executive tracking of [OKR at
section @sec-okr] and should be used for all strategic reporting and governance
discussions related to this initiative.
